message:
fuzz: add fuzz_eulerian_chordal target with rich invariants

New structure-aware fuzz target exercising:

- fnx_algorithms::is_eulerian
- fnx_algorithms::has_eulerian_path
- fnx_algorithms::is_semieulerian
- fnx_algorithms::eulerian_circuit (Hierholzer)
- fnx_algorithms::eulerian_path
- fnx_algorithms::is_chordal
- fnx_algorithms::chordal_graph_cliques

Beyond the no-panic invariant, asserts these runtime contracts:

Eulerian family
  - Determinism: every predicate stable across two calls.
  - Path-vs-circuit: is_eulerian ⇒ has_eulerian_path.
  - Semi-Eulerian disjoint from full Eulerian: when
    is_semieulerian is true, is_eulerian must be false.
  - Circuit length: when a circuit exists, eulerian_circuit
    returns exactly |E| edges (canonical undirected count).
  - Edge coverage: every undirected edge appears at least
    once in the produced circuit (canonical-pair check).
  - Path well-formedness: when has_eulerian_path is true,
    eulerian_path returns |E| edges and covers every edge.

Chordal family
  - Determinism: is_chordal stable across calls.
  - Small-graph baseline: graphs with n ≤ 3 are chordal
    (no cycle of length ≥ 4 exists).
  - chordal_graph_cliques returns pairwise-distinct cliques
    (no duplicate clique entry — sorted-tuple dedup check).

Includes a coverage-minimized seed corpus of 376 inputs
harvested from a 60s libfuzzer pass + merge=1 minimization
(2103 cov edges, 6963 features). Target was registered in
fuzz/Cargo.toml, built clean, ran 32000 iterations across
both passes without invariant violations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+doc = false
